ihc_recommended.rationale: Confirms colorectal lineage; typically positive in colorectal adenocarcinoma.
Intestinal differentiation marker; supports colorectal origin and excludes non-GI primary.
MMR panel — screens for Lynch syndrome and predicts MSI-H status (pembrolizumab eligibility).
MMR panel — pairs with MSH6; loss indicates dMMR/MSI-H.
MMR panel — isolated loss suggests MSH6 germline mutation.
MMR panel — isolated PMS2 loss suggests PMS2 germline mutation; paired loss with MLH1 suggests MLH1 promoter hypermethylation.
Per QC reviewer — assess aberrant p53 expression given areas of less differentiation; supports prognostic stratification.
Confirm or exclude suspected lymphovascular invasion (D2-40 for lymphatic, CD31 for vascular).

molecular_profiling_recommended.test: Extended RAS panel (KRAS exons 2/3/4, NRAS exons 2/3/4)
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Required for anti-EGFR eligibility (cetuximab/panitumumab) in metastatic CRC per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024; RAS-mutant tumors are ineligible.
Strong prognostic marker; BRAF V600E-mutant mCRC qualifies for encorafenib + cetuximab per NCCN; impacts Lynch screening algorithm when paired with MLH1 loss.

molecular_profiling_recommended.method: MMR IHC (4-protein panel) and/or MSI PCR / NGS
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: MSI-H/dMMR tumors qualify for first-line pembrolizumab in metastatic disease (KEYNOTE-177); also identifies Lynch syndrome candidates.
HER2-amplified RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC eligible for trastuzumab + tucatinib/pertuzumab/lapatinib per NCCN.
Rare but actionable — larotrectinib/entrectinib for NTRK-fusion-positive mCRC.
Identifies additional actionable alterations (POLE, KRAS G12C, etc.) and supports clinical trial enrollment in Stage IV disease.

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT3 pN1 with clinical Stage IVA (M1a) per provided clinical staging
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: For Stage IV disease, perioperative/adjuvant strategy is individualized: systemic chemotherapy with FOLFOX or CAPOX is standard backbone for fit patients with resectable oligometastatic disease per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024; duration (3 vs 6 months) and addition of biologics depends on RAS/BRAF/MSI status and metastatic site resectability.
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: If RAS wild-type AND BRAF wild-type: anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab or panitumumab) plus chemotherapy is appropriate, particularly for left-sided primaries (sigmoid colon qualifies). If BRAF V600E-mutant: consider encorafenib + cetuximab in later lines. If HER2-amplified and RAS/BRAF wild-type: trastuzumab-based regimens.
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: If MSI-H / dMMR confirmed: first-line pembrolizumab is preferred per NCCN, supported by KEYNOTE-177 (median PFS 16.5 vs 8.2 months vs chemotherapy). MMR IHC currently pending — defer immunotherapy decision until results available.
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. These recommendations are based on pathologic findings only, follow NCCN Colon Cancer Guidelines v2.2024, and do not substitute for full clinical evaluation, restaging imaging, performance status assessment, or molecular profiling results.

cap_report.comment: Sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated (WHO G2), with provided clinical staging of pT3 pN1 M1a (Stage IVA). Grade was downgraded from the Differential-Diagnostician's initial G3 to G2 following Quality-Control review and the Cross-Slide Aggregator's consolidated assessment, which found the dominant pattern to remain glandular with moderate (not severe) nuclear pleomorphism across slides 1 and 2. Suspected lymphovascular invasion requires confirmation with D2-40 and CD31 IHC. MMR IHC and extended RAS/BRAF testing are essential to determine first-line therapy in the Stage IV setting per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024 — particularly to identify candidates for first-line pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-177) versus cytotoxic chemotherapy ± anti-EGFR backbone. The literature retrieval returned predominantly pancreatic cancer cases that are not directly applicable to this colorectal primary; recommendations therefore rely on standard NCCN/AJCC guidance rather than case-matched references. Final treatment decisions require multidisciplinary tumor board review.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

Lymphovascular invasion only suspected — D2-40/CD31 IHC required for confirmation
Perineural invasion not assessed on submitted ROIs
Tumor budding not assessable — invasive front not represented
Mitotic count and necrosis percent not quantifiable on submitted ROIs

MMR/MSI status pending — required before immunotherapy decision
Histo-A mean confidence 0.567 is moderate; Histo-B numeric confidence unavailable
Slide 0 contained no diagnostic tissue (Histo-A confidence 0.00)
Literature corpus returned predominantly pancreatic cases — case-matched evidence not available
cap_report.thinking: Patient TCGA-F4-6809 is a TCGA-COAD case with sigmoid colon primary and provided clinical staging pT3 pN1, clinical Stage IVA (implying M1a). Three slides were submitted; slide 0 had no diagnostic tissue, while slides 1 and 2 demonstrated glandular adenocarcinoma with desmoplastic stroma, moderate nuclear pleomorphism (score 2), prominent nucleoli, and clumped chromatin. The Differential-Diagnostician initially assigned G3 citing focal less-differentiated areas, but Quality-Control challenged this and the Cross-Slide Aggregator consolidated the grade as G2 with no grade heterogeneity — I therefore report WHO G2 (moderately differentiated) as the final grade and rank moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma as the leading diagnosis (probability 0.70), retaining poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma as the second-ranked differential (0.20) to acknowledge the focal high-grade areas. Mucinous adenocarcinoma is included as a low-probability third differential. Staging follows AJCC 8th edition: pT3 pN1 M1a corresponds to Stage IVA. ICD-O-3 is 8140/3 (adenocarcinoma NOS) since the dominant pattern is glandular and no special subtype was identified. IHC panel includes CK20/CDX2 for lineage, full 4-protein MMR panel for Lynch/MSI screening, p53 per QC's request given the focal high-grade areas, and D2-40/CD31 to confirm the suspected LVI. Molecular profiling prioritizes extended RAS, BRAF V600E, and MMR/MSI as Tier 1 standard of care for metastatic CRC per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024, with HER2 and CGP as Tier 2 and NTRK as Tier 3. Treatment implications focus on the Stage IV setting: FOLFOX/CAPOX backbone, anti-EGFR if RAS/BRAF wild-type (favored for left-sided sigmoid primary), encorafenib + cetuximab if BRAF V600E, and pembrolizumab first-line if MSI-H per KEYNOTE-177. Pipeline confidence is set to 0.65 to match the QC agent's assessment. Inter-reader agreement is reported as not numerically applicable since Histo-B confidence was not provided. Uncertainty flags capture the grade discordance, unconfirmed LVI, missing PNI/budding/mitotic/necrosis assessments, pending MMR results, and the literature mismatch.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the grade heterogeneity noted by Histo-B and the presence of less differentiated areas, the primary diagnosis was revised to poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (G3). The suspected lymphovascular invasion requires further confirmation, and the margin status is close but not involved according to Histo-B. The differential includes moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, but the less differentiated areas favor the G3 assignment. The recommended IHC panel matches the diagnosis of colorectal adenocarcinoma.

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with perineural invasion and desmoplastic stromal reaction, which could be considered for differential diagnosis despite being unrelated to colorectal cancer.

cap_report.comment: Colorectal adenocarcinoma with mixed G2/G3 morphology and suspected lymphovascular invasion. The case demonstrates grade heterogeneity that warrants the higher-grade designation per WHO 5th edition recommendations (highest grade present determines overall grade in colorectal carcinoma). Universal MMR testing is mandatory per NCCN CRC v2.2024 and Lynch syndrome screening guidelines (Giardiello et al., Am J Gastroenterol 2014; NCCN Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment: Colorectal v3.2024). Extended RAS and BRAF V600E testing should be performed reflexively or at the time of metastatic diagnosis to inform systemic therapy selection (Sepulveda et al., J Mol Diagn 2017 — CAP/AMP/ASCP/ASCO molecular testing guideline). Tumor budding (ITBCC 2016, Lugli et al., Mod Pathol 2017) is an independent prognostic marker in Stage II disease and should be formally assessed on H&E at the invasive front when full sections are available. Final staging requires evaluation of regional lymph nodes (minimum 12 nodes recommended per NCCN/CAP) and accurate measurement of distance to the radial/circumferential margin. Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion is recommended before therapeutic decisions.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

cap_report.thinking: Patient TCGA-5M-AAT6 belongs to the TCGA-COAD colorectal adenocarcinoma cohort, anchoring the diagnosis to colon, not breast or other organs. Two slides were reviewed; both contain adenocarcinoma with glandular architecture, moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, clumped chromatin, desmoplastic stroma, and suspected LVI. The Differential-Diagnostician revised the consolidated G2 from the Cross-Slide Aggregator to a mixed G2/G3 after QC raised grade-heterogeneity and mitotic-count discordance concerns; per WHO 5th edition, when grade heterogeneity exists, the highest grade present is reported, supporting the mixed G2/G3 designation. Staging is provisional pT3 based on described invasive features, but pN cannot be assessed since lymph nodes were not submitted, so the AJCC 8th edition stage group cannot be finalized — flagged as Stage II vs III pending nodal evaluation. Margins are reported qualitatively as close but uninvolved (Synthesis B). Per NCCN CRC v2.2024, universal MMR testing, extended RAS, and BRAF V600E are Tier 1 standard-of-care molecular tests; HER2 and NTRK are Tier 2/3. Treatment recommendations follow NCCN-stratified pathways with explicit MDT caveat. QC requested p53 IHC for high-grade morphology, which is included; BRCA1 germline was also requested but is not standard for CRC and was therefore not added to IHC (germline testing is a clinical/genetics referral, not pathology IHC). Inter-reader agreement is not computable as Histo-B confidence was not provided, and evidence quality is moderate given only two slides and no nodal sampling. The colorectal grading scheme (WHO G1-G3) was used — SBR/Nottingham was explicitly avoided because that scheme applies only to breast carcinoma.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, which could serve as a differential diagnosis when considering glandular patterns.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
suggested_papers.relevance: This case highlights the importance of immunohistochemistry in distinguishing between different types of pancreatic tumors, which might be indirectly useful for understanding diagnostic nuances.

clinical_data.note: Colorectal adenocarcinoma cohort. Use colorectal grading (WHO G1-G3).

report.debate_summary: Diagnostician proposed Colon Adenocarcinoma. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must clarify grade assignment considering grade heterogeneity and subtle acinar variant. Literature-Hunter needs to provide relevant references for colorectal adenocarcinoma.

specimen.type: Cecum, colectomy specimen — H&E stained slides

cap_report.diagnosis_line: CECUM, COLECTOMY: ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (G2), INVASIVE THROUGH MUSCULARIS PROPRIA INTO PERICOLIC TISSUE (pT3), WITH REGIONAL LYMPH NODE METASTASIS (pN1)

synoptic.histologic_type: Adenocarcinoma, NOS, glandular pattern with subtle acinar variant (ICD-O-3: 8140/3)
synoptic.grade: G2 — moderately differentiated (WHO 2019, colorectal); grade heterogeneity noted
synoptic.mitotic_count: Not assessable — mitotic count not quantified on submitted slides
synoptic.tumor_budding: Not assessable — dedicated peritumoral budding hotspot evaluation per ITBCC 2016 not performed on submitted ROIs

margins.summary: Closest margin is close but not involved by tumor; specific margin distance not measurable from submitted slides

synoptic.pt: pT3 — tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues
synoptic.pn: pN1 — metastasis in 1-3 regional lymph nodes (per provided clinical staging)
synoptic.pm: Not applicable — no distant metastatic material submitted
synoptic.stage_group: Stage IIIB (AJCC 8th edition, colon: T3 N1 M0)

ihc_recommended.rationale: Confirm colorectal lineage (typically CK20+/CK7-)
Intestinal differentiation marker; supports colorectal origin
MMR screening; loss suggests Lynch syndrome or sporadic MLH1 hypermethylation
MMR screening; loss is highly suggestive of Lynch syndrome
MMR screening; isolated loss seen in Lynch syndrome
MMR screening; complements MLH1 evaluation
QC-requested workup; aberrant pattern correlates with TP53 mutation and may inform prognostication given grade heterogeneity

molecular_profiling_recommended.test: KRAS (exons 2, 3, 4) and NRAS (exons 2, 3, 4) extended mutation panel
molecular_profiling_recommended.method: NGS panel (preferred) or PCR-based hotspot assay
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Required for anti-EGFR therapy eligibility (cetuximab/panitumumab) per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024; RAS mutations confer resistance

molecular_profiling_recommended.method: NGS panel or IHC (VE1 clone) with PCR confirmation
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Prognostic and predictive marker; BRAF V600E mutation confers poor prognosis and informs encorafenib + cetuximab eligibility per NCCN CRC v2.2024

molecular_profiling_recommended.method: MMR IHC (4-protein panel) and/or MSI PCR/NGS
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Universal screening for Lynch syndrome and predictive biomarker for immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (pembrolizumab) per KEYNOTE-177 and NCCN CRC v2.2024

molecular_profiling_recommended.method: IHC with FISH reflex, or NGS copy number analysis
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: HER2-amplified metastatic CRC may benefit from trastuzumab + tucatinib/lapatinib per NCCN CRC v2.2024 (later-line setting)

molecular_profiling_recommended.test: Comprehensive solid tumor NGS panel (incl. NTRK, RET, POLE/POLD1)
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Identification of rare actionable alterations (NTRK fusions → larotrectinib/entrectinib; POLE ultramutated tumors may respond to immunotherapy)

differential_diagnosis.diagnosis: Colorectal adenocarcinoma, NOS, moderately differentiated (G2), cecal origin
key_supporting: Dominant glandular architecture in tumor-bearing slides (0, 1)
Moderate nuclear pleomorphism (grade 2) with prominent nucleoli and clumped chromatin
Desmoplastic stromal reaction supporting invasive carcinoma
Suspected lymphovascular invasion
Cohort and clinical context (TCGA-COAD, cecum) consistent with colorectal primary
key_against: Mitotic count not quantified
MMR/molecular status pending — cannot fully subtype

key_supporting: Cecal location has higher baseline incidence of mucinous histology
Focal mucin production may be present but not quantified
key_against: Dominant pattern across ROIs is glandular, not mucinous
No significant extracellular mucin pools (>50% of tumor volume) reported by upstream agents

key_supporting: Cecal location is the most common site for medullary carcinoma
Frequently associated with MSI-H/dMMR phenotype — to be excluded by MMR IHC
key_against: Reported architecture is glandular rather than solid sheets with pushing borders
No prominent peritumoral lymphocytic infiltrate described

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT3 pN1 cM0 — Stage IIIB (AJCC 8th edition, colon)
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Adjuvant oxaliplatin-based combination chemotherapy (FOLFOX or CAPOX) for 3-6 months per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024 and IDEA collaboration data; duration tailored to risk (T3N1 typically eligible for 3 months CAPOX in low-risk subset)
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: Anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab or panitumumab) is reserved for metastatic, RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided primaries; cecal (right-sided) tumors derive less benefit from anti-EGFR even if RAS WT — molecular results required
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: If MSI-H/dMMR confirmed, pembrolizumab is first-line standard for unresectable/metastatic disease per KEYNOTE-177 (NCCN CRC v2.2024); MMR IHC pending
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. These recommendations are based on pathologic findings only and do not substitute for full clinical evaluation.

per_slide_summary.dominant_pattern: Glandular adenocarcinoma with subtle acinar variant
Fibrous stroma with scattered inflammatory cells
key_findings: No evidence of malignancy in submitted ROIs

quality_metrics.inter_reader_agreement: Not applicable — single histopathologist reader (Histo-A); Histo-B output not available
quality_metrics.evidence_quality: Moderate — limited ROIs across 3 slides with 2 tumor-bearing; mitotic count and budding not quantified

cap_report.comment: Cecal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated, with grade heterogeneity and a subtle acinar variant, staged as pT3 pN1 (AJCC Stage IIIB). Right-sided (cecal) primaries warrant universal MMR/MSI screening given the higher prevalence of dMMR/MSI-H phenotype in proximal colon tumors and its therapeutic implications (KEYNOTE-177; André et al., NEJM 2020). Comprehensive RAS/BRAF testing is required prior to consideration of any anti-EGFR therapy in the metastatic setting (Van Cutsem et al., Ann Oncol 2016; NCCN CRC v2.2024). Adjuvant chemotherapy duration should be guided by the IDEA pooled analysis (Grothey et al., NEJM 2018). Mitotic count, peritumoral tumor budding (ITBCC 2016), perineural invasion, and definitive margin distances should be confirmed on additional sections by the reviewing pathologist. Final diagnosis is contingent on review of all glass slides and ancillary studies by a board-certified pathologist.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.
uncertainty_flags: QC verdict was revision_requested — grade assignment inconsistency flagged across slides
Mitotic count not quantified — required for full CAP synoptic compliance
Tumor budding not assessed per ITBCC 2016
Perineural invasion not evaluated
Lymphovascular invasion only suspected, not definitively confirmed
Margin distances not measurable from submitted ROIs
MMR/MSI status pending — affects Lynch screening, prognosis, and immunotherapy eligibility
RAS/BRAF status pending — required for targeted therapy decisions
Single-reader assessment (Histo-B unavailable) — no inter-reader agreement metric
Literature retrieval returned predominantly non-colorectal references — clinical comment relies on standard NCCN/landmark trial citations rather than retrieved corpus
cap_report.thinking: Clinical context anchors the case to cecum (TCGA-COAD) with pT3 pN1, mandating colorectal grading (WHO G1-G3) and AJCC 8th edition colon staging — yielding Stage IIIB. Cross-slide synthesis confirms G2 glandular adenocarcinoma in slides 0 and 1 with grade heterogeneity and a subtle acinar variant, while slide 2 is non-tumor stroma. Differential diagnosis prioritizes conventional colorectal adenocarcinoma (0.75) with mucinous and medullary variants as alternatives given cecal predilection. Molecular workup follows NCCN CRC v2.2024 standard-of-care: RAS/BRAF/MMR as Tier 1, HER2 as Tier 2. QC flagged grade inconsistency and recommended additional p53 IHC, which is incorporated. Confidence is set to 0.65 to mirror QC's assessment, with explicit uncertainty flags for unquantified mitoses, budding, PNI, and pending molecular results. Right-sided primary location is emphasized in treatment implications because anti-EGFR efficacy and MSI prevalence both differ from left-sided CRC.

report.report_html:
CECUM, COLECTOMY: ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (G2), INVASIVE THROUGH MUSCULARIS PROPRIA INTO PERICOLIC TISSUE (pT3), WITH REGIONAL LYMPH NODE METASTASIS (pN1)

Synoptic Report

Histologic TypeAdenocarcinoma, NOS, glandular pattern with subtle acinar variant (ICD-O-3: 8140/3)
GradeG2 — moderately differentiated (WHO 2019, colorectal); grade heterogeneity noted
Tumor Size MmNone
Mitotic CountNot assessable — mitotic count not quantified on submitted slides
Necrosis PercentNone
Tumor BuddingNot assessable — dedicated peritumoral budding hotspot evaluation per ITBCC 2016 not performed on submitted ROIs
Lymphovascular InvasionSuspected
Perineural InvasionNot assessed
Margins{"radial_margin": "Close but not involved", "proximal_margin": "Not assessed", "distal_margin": "Not assessed", "summary": "Closest margin is close but not involved by tumor; specific margin distance not measurable from submitted slides"}
PtpT3 — tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues
PnpN1 — metastasis in 1-3 regional lymph nodes (per provided clinical staging)
PmNot applicable — no distant metastatic material submitted
Stage GroupStage IIIB (AJCC 8th edition, colon: T3 N1 M0)
Msi PredictedNot predicted — MMR IHC (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) pending

IHC Panel (Recommended)

  • MSH2 — recommended — result pending
  • MSH6 — recommended — result pending
  • PMS2 — recommended — result pending
  • p53 — recommended — result pending

QC Debate Summary

Verdict: revision_requested | Challenges:

  • [MAJOR] Grade assignment inconsistency between slides
  • [MAJOR] Irrelevant literature references

Diagnostician proposed Colon Adenocarcinoma. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must clarify grade assignment considering grade heterogeneity and subtle acinar variant. Literature-Hunter needs to provide relevant references for colorectal adenocarcinoma.

Comment

Cecal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated, with grade heterogeneity and a subtle acinar variant, staged as pT3 pN1 (AJCC Stage IIIB). Right-sided (cecal) primaries warrant universal MMR/MSI screening given the higher prevalence of dMMR/MSI-H phenotype in proximal colon tumors and its therapeutic implications (KEYNOTE-177; André et al., NEJM 2020). Comprehensive RAS/BRAF testing is required prior to consideration of any anti-EGFR therapy in the metastatic setting (Van Cutsem et al., Ann Oncol 2016; NCCN CRC v2.2024). Adjuvant chemotherapy duration should be guided by the IDEA pooled analysis (Grothey et al., NEJM 2018). Mitotic count, peritumoral tumor budding (ITBCC 2016), perineural invasion, and definitive margin distances should be confirmed on additional sections by the reviewing pathologist. Final diagnosis is contingent on review of all glass slides and ancillary studies by a board-certified pathologist.

AI-assisted analysis (PathMind v0.2 · AMD MI300X · 2026-05-08). Must be reviewed by a licensed pathologist before clinical use.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the presence of grade heterogeneity and the subtle acinar variant, the primary diagnosis has been revised to G2 (moderately differentiated) colon adenocarcinoma. The supporting evidence includes moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, clumped chromatin, suspected lymphovascular invasion, and desmoplastic stromal reaction. The differential diagnosis includes mucinous adenocarcinoma, although the dominant pattern is glandular. The recommended IHC panel includes CK20, CDX2, MMR (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2), and KRAS/NRAS/BRAF to further confirm the diagnosis.

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT3 pN2 M0 — AJCC 8th edition Stage IIIC (cecum)
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Adjuvant chemotherapy indicated for Stage III colon cancer per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024: FOLFOX (5-FU/leucovorin/oxaliplatin) or CAPOX (capecitabine/oxaliplatin) for 3–6 months. IDEA collaboration data support 6-month duration for high-risk Stage III (T4 or N2), so 6-month CAPOX or FOLFOX is preferred for this pT3 N2 case.
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: Anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab/panitumumab) is reserved for metastatic disease and requires confirmed RAS-WT and BRAF-WT status; not indicated in adjuvant setting. Encorafenib + cetuximab considered in BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic CRC.
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: If MSI-H/dMMR confirmed on MMR IHC, pembrolizumab is first-line for metastatic CRC (KEYNOTE-177). Adjuvant immunotherapy in dMMR Stage III is under investigation (ATOMIC trial); not yet standard. MMR IHC pending.
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. These recommendations are based on pathologic findings only and do not substitute for full clinical evaluation. NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024 referenced.

cap_report.comment: This Stage IIIC (pT3 pN2) cecal adenocarcinoma represents high-risk node-positive colon cancer for which adjuvant oxaliplatin-based combination chemotherapy is standard of care (NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024). Right-sided/cecal tumors have an elevated prevalence of dMMR/MSI-H (approximately 20%) and BRAF V600E mutations relative to left-sided primaries; both molecular features carry prognostic and therapeutic implications, making MMR IHC and extended RAS/BRAF testing essential. The QC review identified residual disagreement between Histo-A and Histo-B regarding focal high-grade features; the consolidated assessment supports G2 but the case warrants pathologist re-review of any heterogeneous areas before sign-out. Margin status reported as close but not involved — recommend correlation with gross specimen and explicit measurement of closest radial margin distance. Tumor budding could not be assessed from the submitted ROIs and should be evaluated on the original H&E (ITBCC 2016 criteria) given its independent prognostic value in colorectal carcinoma. Lynch syndrome screening via universal MMR IHC is recommended (NCCN Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment v1.2024).
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

cap_report.thinking: Anchored to clinical_context (Cecum, TCGA-COAD) — used WHO colorectal grading (G1–G3), AJCC 8th edition colon TNM, and ICD-O-3 8140/3 for adenocarcinoma NOS (NOT 8500/3 which is breast). Cross-Slide Aggregator consolidated G2 while Differential-Diagnostician revised to G3 based on Histo-B; QC flagged this inconsistency. I retained the consolidated G2 as the primary grade because (1) the aggregator integrates both slides, (2) Histo-B's confidence was not quantified, and (3) glandular architecture predominates. Focal possible G3 features are flagged in the comment and uncertainty list rather than upgrading the whole case. pT3 N2 from clinical input maps to AJCC 8th Stage IIIC (consistent with provided clinical_stage). MSI not predicted in silico — flagged as IHC pending. Treatment recommendations follow NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024 for high-risk Stage III (6-month oxaliplatin doublet). Top-3 DDx ranked by morphology: adenocarcinoma G2 > G3 > mucinous variant (cecal location bias). All IHC marked recommended/pending per legal constraint. Confidence set to 0.70 reflecting QC verdict (0.65) and aggregator confidence (0.85) midpoint, accounting for unresolved grade discordance.

Right-sided/cecal tumors have an elevated prevalence of dMMR/MSI-H (approximately 20%) and BRAF V600E mutations relative to left-sided primaries; both molecular features carry prognostic and therapeutic implications, making MMR IHC and extended RAS/BRAF testing essential. debate_rounds.argument: Given the presence of possible G3 features in some areas as suggested by Histo-B and the subtle acinar variant noted, the primary diagnosis has been revised to G3 (poorly differentiated) to account for these findings. The margin status has been reconciled based on the available data from Histo-B, indicating margins are close but not involved. The differential diagnosis remains consistent with colon adenocarcinoma, but the presence of possible G3 features suggests a higher grade than G2.

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT3 pN2 (cM0 not reported) — AJCC 8th edition Stage IIIC
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Adjuvant oxaliplatin-based combination chemotherapy is standard of care for Stage IIIC colon cancer: FOLFOX or CAPOX for 3–6 months per NCCN CRC v2.2024 and IDEA collaboration data; for high-risk Stage III (T4 and/or N2), 6 months is generally preferred over 3 months
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: Targeted therapy is not indicated in the adjuvant setting. In the event of metastatic recurrence: anti-EGFR (cetuximab or panitumumab) is appropriate ONLY if RAS/BRAF wild-type and left-sided primary; encorafenib + cetuximab if BRAF V600E mutated (mCRC); HER2-directed therapy if ERBB2 amplified and RAS/BRAF wild-type
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: If MSI-H / dMMR is confirmed on pending MMR IHC, pembrolizumab is first-line standard for metastatic disease per KEYNOTE-177; in localized dMMR Stage III disease, neoadjuvant/adjuvant immunotherapy is increasingly considered (NICHE-2 and ongoing trials) — refer to multidisciplinary tumor board
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. cap_report.comment: Stage IIIC (pT3 pN2) colon adenocarcinoma with cribriform/glandular morphology and documented grade heterogeneity (consolidated G2 with focal G3 and subtle acinar variants). Per NCCN CRC v2.2024, all newly diagnosed colorectal adenocarcinomas should undergo universal MMR IHC screening (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) for Lynch syndrome and to inform immunotherapy candidacy. Extended RAS and BRAF V600E testing is required to inform systemic therapy decisions in the event of recurrence. Adjuvant oxaliplatin-based combination chemotherapy (FOLFOX or CAPOX) is the standard of care for Stage IIIC disease; per IDEA collaboration analyses (Grothey et al., NEJM 2018), 6 months of therapy is preferred over 3 months for high-risk Stage III (T4 and/or N2). p53 IHC is recommended per QC review given focal high-grade morphology. Tumor budding per ITBCC 2016 (Lugli et al., Mod Pathol 2017) should be scored on H&E sections at a defined hotspot at 20× magnification when slides are reviewed at the bench. Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion is required.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

cap_report.thinking: Clinical context anchors this case to colon (TCGA-COAD), so all staging, grading, and treatment frames use AJCC 8th edition colon and WHO G1–G3 (not SBR or Gleason). Cross-Slide Aggregator consolidated grade was G2 with documented heterogeneity; Differential-Diagnostician escalated to G3 citing acinar variants and prominent nucleoli; QC challenged the G3 escalation because the majority of tumor-bearing slides showed G2. I reconciled by reporting consolidated WHO G2 with focal G3 features and explicit grade heterogeneity, which honors both the majority morphology and the QC-flagged discordance. Clinical T3 N2 maps to Stage IIIC (AJCC 8th ed.) assuming M0. Treatment framing follows NCCN CRC v2.2024 with the mandatory multidisciplinary caveat. Pipeline confidence is set to 0.65 to match QC confidence rather than the higher Differential-Diagnostician confidence, per the rule that pipeline confidence must match QC. Molecular workup reflects standard CRC tier-1 testing (RAS/BRAF/MMR), tier-2 HER2, and tier-3 broad NGS. ICD-O 8201/3 (cribriform comedo-type adenocarcinoma) was selected as the closest WHO morphology code, with 8140/3 (adenocarcinoma NOS) noted as the conservative default.

synoptic.tumor_budding: Not assessable — dedicated peritumoral hotspot evaluation per ITBCC 2016 not performed on submitted ROIs

margins.radial_circumferential: Clear but close — further evaluation recommended
margins.summary: Margins clear but close; deeper levels and/or re-orientation recommended to confirm R0 status

synoptic.pt: pT2 — tumor invades muscularis propria (AJCC 8th ed., colon)
synoptic.pn: pN0 — no regional lymph node metastasis (per provided clinical data; ≥12 nodes recommended for adequate staging)
synoptic.pm: Not applicable — distant metastasis not assessed on resection specimen
synoptic.stage_group: Stage I (pT2 N0 M0) — AJCC 8th edition, colon

ihc_recommended.rationale: Universal MMR screening for all colorectal carcinomas (NCCN/CAP)
Component of 4-marker MMR panel; Lynch syndrome screening
Detects isolated MSH6 loss, Lynch-associated
Detects isolated PMS2 loss; pairs with MLH1
Confirms colorectal lineage; prognostic in stage II disease
Supports colorectal phenotype in lineage panel
Helps exclude upper-GI/biliary primary (typical CRC: CK20+/CK7-)
Suggested by QC given focal G3 areas; aberrant pattern correlates with TP53 mutation and adverse prognosis
Highly specific colorectal marker; useful when CK20/CDX2 equivocal

molecular_profiling_recommended.test: Mismatch repair / MSI status (MMR IHC ± MSI-PCR or NGS-based MSI)
molecular_profiling_recommended.method: IHC (4-marker) with reflex MSI-PCR or NGS
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Universal screening per NCCN CRC v2.2024 and Lynch syndrome guidelines; MSI-H/dMMR identifies candidates for immune checkpoint inhibitors and modifies adjuvant 5-FU decisions in stage II disease

molecular_profiling_recommended.test: Extended RAS panel (KRAS exons 2/3/4, NRAS exons 2/3/4)
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Required for anti-EGFR (cetuximab/panitumumab) eligibility in metastatic/recurrent setting per NCCN CRC v2.2024; baseline characterization recommended at diagnosis

molecular_profiling_recommended.method: NGS or PCR (with reflex IHC VE1)
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Prognostic marker; if MLH1 lost on IHC, BRAF V600E supports sporadic (non-Lynch) etiology; eligibility for encorafenib + cetuximab in advanced disease (NCCN CRC v2.2024)

molecular_profiling_recommended.method: IHC with reflex FISH, or NGS copy-number
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Tucatinib + trastuzumab approved for HER2-amplified RAS-WT mCRC; informs second-line options (NCCN CRC v2.2024)

molecular_profiling_recommended.test: Comprehensive solid-tumor NGS panel (NTRK fusions, POLE/POLD1, PIK3CA, SMAD4, APC, TP53)
molecular_profiling_recommended.clinical_rationale: Identifies actionable fusions (larotrectinib/entrectinib for NTRK), ultramutated tumors (POLE) eligible for immunotherapy, and provides prognostic stratification
Emerging tool for minimal residual disease detection in stage I-III colon cancer; may guide adjuvant therapy decisions (NCCN CRC v2.2024 — under evaluation)

differential_diagnosis.diagnosis: Invasive adenocarcinoma of the ascending colon, NOS (WHO G2 with focal G3)
key_supporting: Glandular architecture across all three slides

Desmoplastic stromal reaction
Suspected lymphovascular invasion
Anatomic site (ascending colon) and TCGA-COAD cohort context
Multifocally consistent morphology across slides 0, 1, and 2
key_against: Mitotic count and percent necrosis not quantified on available ROIs

key_supporting: Subtle acinar variant patterns may overlap with mucinous differentiation
key_against: No extracellular mucin pools described (>50% threshold not met)
Dominant pattern is glandular, not mucinous lakes

differential_diagnosis.diagnosis: Metastatic adenocarcinoma to colon (e.g., upper-GI or pancreaticobiliary primary)
key_supporting: Glandular adenocarcinoma morphology is non-specific by H&E alone
key_against: TCGA-COAD cohort assignment with documented colonic primary
Ascending colon mass favors primary CRC
Lineage IHC (CDX2/CK20/SATB2) would clarify and is recommended

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT2 pN0 (cM0 inferred) — AJCC 8th Stage I, ascending colon
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Stage I colon cancer (pT2 N0): adjuvant chemotherapy NOT routinely indicated per NCCN CRC v2.2024. Surveillance is standard. If final margins are confirmed R0 and ≥12 nodes were examined, observation is appropriate.
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: No targeted therapy indicated in stage I disease. Baseline RAS/BRAF/HER2 characterization recommended for future reference should recurrence occur; anti-EGFR (cetuximab/panitumumab) reserved for RAS/BRAF wild-type metastatic disease.
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: Not indicated in stage I. If MSI-H/dMMR is confirmed on pending MMR IHC, pembrolizumab is first-line standard for unresectable/metastatic dMMR CRC per KEYNOTE-177; relevance here is for genetic counseling (Lynch syndrome workup) rather than current treatment.
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. These recommendations are based on pathologic findings only and do not substitute for full clinical evaluation. Confirm adequate lymph node yield (≥12), R0 margin status, and complete MMR/molecular workup before final adjuvant decisions.

per_slide_summary.dominant_pattern: Glandular adenocarcinoma with focal less-differentiated areas
key_findings: Focal areas with reduced glandular formation supporting G3 component

quality_metrics.inter_reader_agreement: Not applicable — single reader (Histo-A); Histo-B output unavailable
quality_metrics.evidence_quality: Moderate — three tumor-positive slides with consistent morphology but limited ROI sampling and no Histo-B cross-validation

cap_report.comment: Three slides from an ascending colon resection demonstrate invasive adenocarcinoma (NOS) with glandular architecture, moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and desmoplastic stromal reaction. Grade is consolidated as WHO G2 with focal G3 areas as flagged on slide 2. Lymphovascular invasion is suspected and should be confirmed on deeper levels and/or with elastic/D2-40 stains. Radial margin is described as clear but close — re-orientation and additional levels are recommended to confirm R0 status. Universal MMR IHC is required for Lynch syndrome screening and informs adjuvant chemotherapy decisions even at stage I. RAS/BRAF/HER2 baseline molecular profiling is recommended for future reference. Per NCCN CRC v2.2024, adjuvant chemotherapy is not routinely indicated for pT2 N0 (Stage I) colon cancer; surveillance per NCCN guidelines is appropriate (Sargent et al., J Clin Oncol 2009; André et al., NEJM 2004 — relevance limited to higher-stage disease). Note: provided literature corpus was largely pancreatic-cancer focused and only marginally relevant; recommendations above derive from current NCCN CRC guidelines.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.
uncertainty_flags: Single-reader output (Histo-B unavailable) — no inter-reader agreement metric
Mitotic count and percent necrosis not quantified on available ROIs
Tumor budding not assessable without dedicated peritumoral hotspot evaluation
Margin status reported as 'clear but close' — deeper levels recommended
Lymphovascular invasion suspected but not definitively confirmed
MMR/MSI status not yet determined — IHC pending
Lymph node yield adequacy (≥12 nodes) not documented in available data
Literature corpus poorly matched to colorectal context
cap_report.thinking: Anchored on clinical_context (ascending colon, TCGA-COAD, Stage I pT2 N0) and upstream agent consensus on colon adenocarcinoma, ICD-O 8140/3. Applied WHO G1-G3 grading (not SBR) and AJCC 8th colon staging. QC debate flagged grade heterogeneity (G2 with focal G3) and close margins; both surfaced in synoptic and comment. Added p53 and SATB2 IHC per QC missing-workup. MSI listed as 'pending' since MMR IHC not yet performed. Treatment recommendations follow NCCN CRC v2.2024 stage I pathway (observation, no adjuvant chemo). Confidence aligned with QC (0.75). Inter-reader agreement marked N/A given single-reader output.

Comment

Three slides from an ascending colon resection demonstrate invasive adenocarcinoma (NOS) with glandular architecture, moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and desmoplastic stromal reaction. Grade is consolidated as WHO G2 with focal G3 areas as flagged on slide 2. Lymphovascular invasion is suspected and should be confirmed on deeper levels and/or with elastic/D2-40 stains. Radial margin is described as clear but close — re-orientation and additional levels are recommended to confirm R0 status. Universal MMR IHC is required for Lynch syndrome screening and informs adjuvant chemotherapy decisions even at stage I. RAS/BRAF/HER2 baseline molecular profiling is recommended for future reference. Per NCCN CRC v2.2024, adjuvant chemotherapy is not routinely indicated for pT2 N0 (Stage I) colon cancer; surveillance per NCCN guidelines is appropriate (Sargent et al., J Clin Oncol 2009; André et al., NEJM 2004 — relevance limited to higher-stage disease). Note: provided literature corpus was largely pancreatic-cancer focused and only marginally relevant; recommendations above derive from current NCCN CRC guidelines.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the QC feedback and the detailed information provided by Synthesis B, the primary diagnosis was revised to include grade heterogeneity and an updated margin status. The presence of focal areas showing less differentiation supports the inclusion of G3 areas within the G2 grade. The margin status is described as clear but close, with further evaluation needed. The differential diagnosis includes mucinous adenocarcinoma, although there is no significant evidence of extracellular mucin pools.
[MAJOR] Grade heterogeneity and margin status inconsistencies
[MINOR] Relevance of literature to the case

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case, despite being pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, shares some histological features like desmoplastic stromal reaction that could be considered for differential diagnosis.

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT3 pN1b cM0 — AJCC Stage IIIB
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Adjuvant chemotherapy indicated for resected Stage III colon cancer (NCCN CRC v2.2024). Standard regimens: FOLFOX or CAPOX × 3-6 months per IDEA collaboration risk stratification (low-risk T1-3/N1: 3 months CAPOX is non-inferior; high-risk T4 or N2: 6 months recommended). Single-agent capecitabine or 5-FU/LV acceptable if oxaliplatin contraindicated.
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: No targeted therapy indicated in the adjuvant setting. In the event of recurrence/metastatic progression: anti-EGFR (cetuximab or panitumumab) for left-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type tumors; encorafenib + cetuximab if BRAF V600E mutated; trastuzumab-based therapy if HER2-amplified RAS/BRAF-WT.
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: If MSI-H/dMMR confirmed on pending MMR IHC: pembrolizumab is preferred 1L therapy in metastatic setting per KEYNOTE-177. Adjuvant immunotherapy in dMMR Stage III is investigational (ATOMIC trial pending mature data) and not standard outside a clinical trial. MMR IHC pending — definitive recommendation deferred.
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. cap_report.comment: This case represents a classic Stage IIIB (pT3 pN1b) sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma in the TCGA-COAD cohort. Universal MMR IHC and extended RAS/BRAF testing are mandated per NCCN CRC v2.2024 to guide both Lynch syndrome screening and metastatic-setting therapy planning. The QC debate flagged grade heterogeneity (subtle G3 acinar features within an overall G2 tumor) and an imprecise margin descriptor ('close but not involved'); the operating pathologist should re-examine deeper levels for definitive margin distance measurement and consider p53 IHC to corroborate any high-grade transformation. Tumor budding (ITBCC 2016) and venous invasion should be formally assessed on H&E and elastin stains respectively, as both are independent prognostic factors in Stage III disease. Adjuvant FOLFOX/CAPOX duration should follow IDEA risk stratification. Reference: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology — Colon Cancer v2.2024; AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th edition; ITBCC 2016 consensus on tumor budding (Lugli et al., Mod Pathol 2017); KEYNOTE-177 (André et al., NEJM 2020).
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

Margin status descriptive ('close but not involved') without quantified distance in millimeters
Mitotic count not quantified per 10 HPF
Tumor budding not assessed per ITBCC 2016 (relevant for colorectal Stage III prognosis)
Lymphovascular invasion suspected but not definitively confirmed
Perineural invasion not assessed
MMR/MSI status pending — immunotherapy eligibility cannot be determined

QC verdict was 'revision_requested' with QC confidence 0.75 — pathologist sign-out required
cap_report.thinking: Anchored to clinical_context (sigmoid colon, TCGA-COAD, Stage IIIB, T3 N1b) and cross-slide synthesis (unifocal glandular adenocarcinoma, G2, desmoplastic stroma, suspected LVI). Used WHO digestive grading (G1-G3) per site rules — not SBR. ICD-O 8140/3 for colonic adenocarcinoma NOS. AJCC 8th edition stage group derived from pT3 + pN1b + cM0 = Stage IIIB. Incorporated QC revision requests by adding p53 IHC and explicitly recommending MMR/MSI testing, and by flagging grade heterogeneity and margin imprecision in uncertainty_flags and comment. Treatment implications follow NCCN CRC v2.2024: FOLFOX/CAPOX adjuvant per IDEA, anti-EGFR/BRAF/HER2 logic deferred to metastatic setting, pembrolizumab pending MMR result. Histo-B confidence is null per input, so inter-reader agreement marked Not applicable rather than fabricated. Pipeline confidence reflects Differential-Diagnostician (0.77) consistent with QC verdict.

report.debate_summary: Diagnostician proposed Colon adenocarcinoma. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must address the discrepancies in tumor presence and grade heterogeneity, and consider additional IHC markers for a more comprehensive workup.

cap_report.diagnosis_line: TRANSVERSE COLON, SEGMENTAL COLECTOMY: ADENOCARCINOMA, MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (WHO G2), INVADING THROUGH THE MUSCULARIS PROPRIA INTO PERICOLIC SOFT TISSUE (pT3); REGIONAL LYMPH NODES NEGATIVE FOR METASTASIS (pN0); MARGINS CLOSE BUT UNINVOLVED.

synoptic.grade: G2 — moderately differentiated (WHO 2019 colorectal grading; >50% gland formation)
synoptic.tumor_budding: Not assessable — dedicated peritumoral hotspot evaluation per ITBCC 2016 not performed on submitted ROIs

margins.summary: All margins free of tumor; circumferential/radial margin reported as close — exact distance not specified in upstream data

synoptic.pt: pT3 — tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues
synoptic.pn: pN0 — no regional lymph node metastasis (per provided clinical staging)
synoptic.pm: Not applicable — distant metastasis not assessed pathologically (cM0 by clinical staging context)
synoptic.stage_group: Stage IIA (AJCC 8th edition: pT3 pN0 cM0)

ihc_recommended.rationale: Confirm colorectal lineage; typically positive in colorectal adenocarcinoma

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT3 pN0 cM0 — AJCC 8th edition Stage IIA colon adenocarcinoma
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Stage IIA disease: observation is acceptable for standard-risk pT3N0 tumors. Adjuvant fluoropyrimidine monotherapy (capecitabine or 5-FU/leucovorin) or FOLFOX/CAPOX should be considered if high-risk features are present (T4, <12 nodes examined, poorly differentiated histology, LVI/PNI, obstruction, perforation, or close/positive margins). Per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024 — final recommendation contingent on MMR/MSI status (MSI-H tumors generally do not benefit from adjuvant 5-FU monotherapy in stage II).
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: Not indicated in the adjuvant stage II setting outside of clinical trials. In the event of recurrence/metastasis: anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab or panitumumab) may be considered if extended RAS and BRAF are wild-type and tumor is left-sided (note: transverse colon — sidedness ambiguous, may behave as right-sided depending on exact location). HER2-directed therapy if HER2 amplified and RAS/BRAF wild-type.
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: If MSI-H/dMMR confirmed: pembrolizumab is first-line standard for unresectable/metastatic disease per KEYNOTE-177. Neoadjuvant dostarlimab/pembrolizumab is being evaluated in dMMR localized disease (NCCN Category 2A in selected settings). MMR IHC pending — defer determination.
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion is required before initiating treatment. These recommendations are derived from pathologic findings only and do not substitute for full clinical evaluation, performance status assessment, comorbidity review, and patient-centered shared decision-making.

quality_metrics.inter_reader_agreement: Moderate (0.60-0.80) — concordance on tumor presence in Slides 0/1 and consolidated G2 grade; discordance regarding subtle acinar features and Slide 2 interpretation
quality_metrics.evidence_quality: Moderate — limited ROIs; two of three slides contain diagnostic tumor; mitotic count, tumor budding, and exact margin distance not formally quantified

cap_report.comment: Moderately differentiated (G2) invasive adenocarcinoma of the transverse colon with pT3 pN0 (Stage IIA) staging. Universal MMR/MSI testing is recommended for all newly diagnosed colorectal carcinomas per NCCN, ASCO, and CAP guidelines, both for Lynch syndrome screening and to inform adjuvant therapy decisions in stage II disease (Sepulveda AR et al., J Clin Oncol 2017;35:1453-1486). For stage II colon cancer with standard-risk features, the absolute benefit of adjuvant chemotherapy is modest (~3-5%) and must be weighed against toxicity (QUASAR Collaborative Group, Lancet 2007;370:2020-2029). Tumor budding (ITBCC 2016) and exact circumferential/radial margin distance should be quantified on definitive review, as these may upstage risk classification. p53 IHC is recommended per QC challenge to further characterize the tumor. RAS/BRAF and MMR/MSI workup should be initiated now to inform treatment in case of recurrence and to complete Lynch syndrome screening.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

cap_report.thinking: Synthesis: TCGA-F4-6570 is a transverse colon (TCGA-COAD cohort) case with clinical pT3N0 Stage IIA. Cross-slide aggregator confirms tumor in Slides 0 and 1 (consolidated G2, glandular pattern, no grade heterogeneity) and no tumor in Slide 2. Differential-Diagnostician favors colon adenocarcinoma NOS (8140/3), G2, with acinar variant as second consideration. QC requested revision citing Slide 2 ambiguity and recommended p53 IHC — these are addressed by the IHC panel and uncertainty flags. Staging follows AJCC 8th colon table: pT3pN0cM0 → Stage IIA. Grading uses WHO colorectal G1-G3 (NOT SBR). Treatment recommendations per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024: adjuvant therapy decision contingent on MMR/MSI and high-risk features. Pipeline confidence set to 0.70, slightly below DDx 0.75 to account for QC revision_requested verdict and missing Histo-B confidence.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the inconsistencies in tumor presence and grade heterogeneity between the two histopathologists, the primary diagnosis of colon adenocarcinoma is supported by the consistent G2 features observed in Slides 0 and 1. However, the discrepancy in Slide 2 and the suggestion of grade heterogeneity by Histo-B introduce ambiguity. The differential diagnosis of colon adenocarcinoma with acinar variants is considered due to the subtle acinar variants mentioned by Histo-B, although this feature is not noted by Histo-A. Further investigation and clarification of these ambiguous features are necessary for a definitive diagnosis.

literature.key_findings: The provided TCGA cases and PubMed articles primarily focus on pancreatic cancer rather than colorectal adenocarcinoma. None of the TCGA cases directly match the transverse colon origin and glandular morphology of the current case. The most relevant paper discusses adjuvant therapy for stage II colon cancer, which could provide some guidance for treatment considerations.

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: While this case involves pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, it highlights the importance of desmoplastic stromal reactions and perineural invasion, which might be considered in the broader context of glandular carcinomas.

treatment_implications.stage_context: pT2 pN0 cM0 — AJCC Stage I, sigmoid colon
treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Stage I colon cancer (pT2 N0): adjuvant chemotherapy is NOT routinely recommended per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024. Surveillance is standard following R0 resection. Adjuvant therapy may be considered only in the setting of high-risk features (e.g., positive margins, inadequate nodal sampling <12 nodes).
treatment_implications.targeted_therapy: Not indicated in stage I disease. In the event of recurrence or metastatic progression: anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab/panitumumab) if left-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type; encorafenib + cetuximab if BRAF V600E mutant; trastuzumab-based regimens if HER2-amplified (RAS/BRAF WT).
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab is first-line standard for dMMR/MSI-H metastatic CRC (KEYNOTE-177). MMR IHC pending — if dMMR confirmed, immunotherapy considerations apply at progression. Not indicated for resected stage I disease.
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. Recommendations follow NCCN Colon Cancer Guidelines v2.2024 and are based on pathologic findings only; they do not substitute for full clinical evaluation. Confirm adequacy of regional lymph node sampling (≥12 nodes) and re-evaluate close radial margin before finalizing surgical adequacy.

per_slide_summary.dominant_pattern: Glandular with areas of cribriform architecture

quality_metrics.inter_reader_agreement: Not applicable — Histopathologist-B confidence not reported; single-reader (Histo-A) confidence available
quality_metrics.evidence_quality: Moderate — three tumor-positive slides with concordant glandular morphology, but limited per-ROI quantitative data (mitotic count, budding, necrosis %) and unresolved margin/grade-heterogeneity discrepancy

cap_report.comment: Three slides from sigmoid colon resection demonstrate a moderately differentiated (G2) adenocarcinoma with predominantly glandular architecture and focal cribriform foci, consistent with TCGA-COAD colorectal adenocarcinoma. Lymphovascular invasion is suspected and warrants targeted re-review on deeper levels or elastic stains. The radial/circumferential margin is reported as close — measurement of the closest distance to the inked margin and re-examination of additional margin sections is recommended before finalizing R-status. Universal MMR IHC (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) is recommended per NCCN Colon Cancer v2.2024 and current USPSTF Lynch screening guidance. Extended RAS (KRAS/NRAS) and BRAF V600E testing should be considered up-front to inform future therapeutic options should disease recur. Per ITBCC 2016, peritumoral tumor budding should be quantified on a dedicated H&E hot-spot at 20× (BD1 0–4 / BD2 5–9 / BD3 ≥10 buds per 0.785 mm²) — not assessable from available ROIs. Adequate nodal yield (≥12 regional nodes) should be confirmed; if not met, consider as a high-risk feature in stage I/II decision-making. Final R-status, definitive grade, and stage assignment should be reconciled at multidisciplinary tumor board.
cap_report.ai_disclosure: This report was generated with AI assistance (PathMind v1.0, powered by AMD MI300X). All findings must be reviewed and validated by a licensed pathologist before clinical use. This report does not constitute a clinical diagnosis.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the architectural complexity and grade heterogeneity noted by Histo-B, the primary diagnosis has been revised to reflect these complexities more accurately. While both histopathologists agreed on the dominant glandular pattern with areas of cribriform architecture, the presence of grade heterogeneity and closer margins suggested by Histo-B necessitates a more cautious approach. The differential diagnosis includes poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma due to the architectural complexity, although the overall glandular architecture predominates, making G2 the more likely grade. Further IHC analysis is recommended to confirm the diagnosis and assess for potential molecular alterations.

The grade was revised to G2 due to the presence of moderately differentiated areas and the predominant pattern. The differential diagnosis includes colorectal adenocarcinoma with an acinar variant, considering the subtle acinar variant reported in Histo-B. The recommended IHC panel matches the actual diagnosis, focusing on colorectal adenocarcinoma.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of solid with glandular differentiation in colorectal adenocarcinoma does not align with the provided TCGA cases, which primarily involve pancreatic adenocarcinoma and neuroendocrine tumors. The closest match is TCGA-IB-7886, a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with grade II-III features, but it is not directly relevant to colorectal cancer. The provided literature focuses on pancreatic cancer, lacking direct applicability to colorectal adenocarcinoma.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
used_papers.relevance: This case represents a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with grade II-III features, providing some morphological insights though not directly applicable to colorectal cancer.

Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
suggested_papers.relevance: This case illustrates a pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2, offering a differential diagnosis perspective that might be useful for comparison.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of solid with focal glandular differentiation (subtle acinar variant) in colorectal adenocarcinoma is not directly supported by the provided TCGA cases, which primarily involve pancreatic tumors. However, the presence of perineural invasion and desmoplastic stromal reaction in TCGA-IB-7886 suggests potential morphological similarities that could be considered in differential diagnosis. The literature does not provide direct evidence for colorectal adenocarcinoma with the specified morphology.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
used_papers.relevance: This case involves pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with features like perineural invasion and desmoplastic stromal reaction that might share some morphological traits with the hypothesized colorectal adenocarcinoma.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal patterns and perineural invasion, which could serve as an alternative differential diagnosis.

debate_history.thinking: Given the grade heterogeneity observed by both histopathologists, the primary diagnosis was revised to G2 (moderately differentiated) to reflect the overall characteristics of the tumor. literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of glandular with some cribriform areas in colorectal adenocarcinoma does not align with the provided TCGA cases, which are primarily pancreatic in origin. None of the TCGA cases directly relate to colorectal adenocarcinoma, suggesting a potential misalignment between the clinical context and the retrieved cases. The PubMed articles also focus on pancreatic adenocarcinoma, lacking direct relevance to colorectal cancer.

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with grade heterogeneity, which might offer insights into grading considerations despite being a different organ.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case, despite being pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, shows a solid growth pattern with desmoplastic stromal reaction, which might offer some morphological insights.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with a multifocal pattern and perineural invasion, providing additional morphological considerations.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case illustrates a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with solid and glandular features, providing a potential differential diagnosis.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
suggested_papers.relevance: This case highlights the importance of immunohistochemistry in distinguishing between pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma and acinar adenocarcinoma, which could be relevant for differential considerations.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) is predicted to become the second leading cause of death in 2030 and it is characterized by poor prognosis, recurrence and resistance to therapies. Several factors contribute

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case highlights the importance of desmoplastic stromal reactions and perineural invasion in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, which could be indirectly informative for understanding similar features in colorectal cancer.

warnings.message: Biomarqueur non reconnu — vérifier l'orthographe IHC.

debate_rounds.argument: The primary diagnosis was revised to incorporate the subtle acinar variant and address grade heterogeneity. The presence of prominent nucleoli, clumped chromatin, and architectural complexity supports a G2 grade. However, the subtle acinar variant and edge cases between G1 and G2 suggest a degree of heterogeneity. The margin status was clarified based on Synthesis B, indicating close but not involved margins. Further IHC analysis is recommended to confirm the diagnosis and assess molecular characteristics.

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

debate_rounds.argument: Given the clinical context of cecal origin and the histopathological findings, the primary diagnosis is colon adenocarcinoma. The dominant glandular pattern with cribriform areas supports this diagnosis. However, the presence of subtle acinar variants and potential focal G3 areas in Histo-B introduces ambiguity regarding grade heterogeneity. Therefore, the grade is revised to G2 with focal G3 areas, acknowledging the observed heterogeneity. The differential includes colon adenocarcinoma with mucinous features, although there is no explicit evidence of mucin pools. Recommended IHC markers align with the diagnosis of colon adenocarcinoma.

literature.key_findings: The provided TCGA cases and PubMed articles predominantly focus on pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) rather than colorectal adenocarcinoma. None of the TCGA cases directly match the cecal origin and glandular with cribriform morphology of the current case. The literature highlights the importance of molecular profiling and grading in PDAC, which may offer indirect insights into similar morphological patterns.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
used_papers.relevance: This case provides information on PDAC with perineural invasion and desmoplastic stromal reaction, offering some morphological parallels.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
used_papers.relevance: This case details a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal patterns and perineural invasion, providing additional morphological context.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

The differential diagnosis includes mucinous colorectal adenocarcinoma, although the lack of explicit mention of mucin production makes this less likely. The recommended IHC panel includes markers specific to colorectal adenocarcinoma.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal pattern and perineural invasion, providing insights into another potential differential diagnosis.

Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar

used_papers.title: Molecular Subtyping and Genomic Profiling Expand Precision Medicine in KRAS Wild-Type Pancreatic Cancer.
used_papers.snippet:
Molecular Subtyping and Genomic Profiling Expand Precision Medicine in KRAS Wild-Type Pancreatic Cancer.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) is a highly lethal disease with poor prognosis and limited treatment options. While the majority of PDAC cases harbor KRAS mutations, approximately 8%-10% are KRAS wild-type

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with desmoplastic stromal reaction and perineural invasion, which might offer insights into similar histological features.

cap_report.comment: The working hypothesis of colorectal adenocarcinoma with a subtle acinar variant is not directly supported by the provided TCGA cases, which primarily involve pancreatic adenocarcinomas. However, the presence of perineural invasion and lymphovascular invasion in the pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma cases suggests aggressive behavior that could be analogous to certain colorectal adenocarcinoma variants. The literature does not provide direct evidence for colorectal adenocarcinoma with an acinar variant.

Further investigation is needed to clarify the grade discrepancy and lymphovascular invasion status.

PtpT3
PnCannot be assessed — lymph nodes not submitted
PmNot applicable
Stage GroupIIA
Msi PredictedNot predicted — MMR IHC pending

IHC Panel (Recommended)

  • CK20 — recommended — result pending
  • CDX2 — recommended — result pending
  • MMR (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2) — recommended — result pending
  • p53 — recommended — result pending
  • E-cadherin — recommended — result pending

QC Debate Summary

Verdict: revision_requested | Challenges:

  • [MAJOR] Grade assignment inconsistency between G2 and G3
  • [MINOR] Ambiguous features not fully addressed

Diagnostician proposed Colorectal adenocarcinoma with subtle acinar variant. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must address the grade discrepancy between G2 and G3 and provide a clear rationale for the final grade assignment. Additionally, the ambiguous features should be fully addressed in the final diagnosis.

Comment

The working hypothesis of colorectal adenocarcinoma with a subtle acinar variant is not directly supported by the provided TCGA cases, which primarily involve pancreatic adenocarcinomas. However, the presence of perineural invasion and lymphovascular invasion in the pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma cases suggests aggressive behavior that could be analogous to certain colorectal adenocarcinoma variants. The literature does not provide direct evidence for colorectal adenocarcinoma with an acinar variant.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
used_papers.relevance: This case highlights aggressive features like perineural and lymphovascular invasion, which may be relevant to understanding the behavior of certain colorectal adenocarcinoma variants.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
used_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with grade heterogeneity and desmoplastic stromal reaction, providing insights into tumor complexity that might be paralleled in some colorectal adenocarcinomas.

debate_rounds.argument: The primary diagnosis has been revised to Colorectal adenocarcinoma, cribriform type, based on the cribriform pattern identified in both Histo-A and Histo-B syntheses. The presence of subtle acinar variants in Histo-B synthesis influenced the grading towards G2, which was not considered in Histo-A. The differential diagnosis includes Colorectal adenocarcinoma, solid type with glandular differentiation, supported by solid with glandular differentiation noted in both syntheses. However, the cribriform pattern is the dominant pattern, and the subtle acinar variant noted in Histo-B synthesis supports the cribriform type over the solid type. The recommended IHC panel includes MMR, KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, CDX2, and CK20, which are relevant for colorectal adenocarcinoma. The ambiguous features include grade heterogeneity and subtle acinar variant influence on grading.

literature.key_findings: The provided TCGA cases and PubMed articles predominantly focus on pancreatic adenocarcinoma rather than colorectal adenocarcinoma.
