suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with desmoplastic stromal reaction and perineural invasion, which could be considered for differential diagnosis.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
suggested_papers.relevance: This case highlights the differential diagnosis between pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma and acinar adenocarcinoma, which might be useful for understanding tumor morphology.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with perineural and lymphovascular invasion, providing insights into tumor behavior.

The primary diagnosis remains colon adenocarcinoma, supported by the observed solid with focal glandular differentiation, prominent nucleoli, and coarse chromatin. The differential diagnosis includes mucinous adenocarcinoma, although the dominant pattern is not mucinous.

None of the TCGA cases directly correspond to the working hypothesis of 'solid with focal glandular differentiation' in colorectal cancer. Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with perineural invasion, offering another potential differential diagnosis for consideration.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with some morphological features that could be considered in a broader differential diagnosis.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal patterns, which might offer insights into similar morphological patterns despite being a different organ.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

debate_rounds.argument: Re-evaluation of the cross-slide data revealed that the architectural complexity and the presence of the subtle acinar variant warrant a higher grade assignment. The solid with focal glandular differentiation pattern, along with prominent nucleoli and coarse chromatin, supports a diagnosis of poorly differentiated colon adenocarcinoma. The desmoplastic stromal reaction further indicates an invasive process. While there is no direct literature support for this specific pattern in colorectal adenocarcinoma, the grade heterogeneity noted in both Histo-A and Histo-B syntheses introduces some uncertainty. However, the overall evidence points towards a higher-grade malignancy.

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
suggested_papers.relevance: This case illustrates the differential diagnosis between pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma and acinar adenocarcinoma, emphasizing the role of immunohistochemistry.

literature.key_findings: The provided TCGA cases and PubMed articles predominantly focus on pancreatic cancer rather than colorectal adenocarcinoma. None of the TCGA cases directly correspond to the ascending colon adenocarcinoma scenario. The most relevant paper discusses adjuvant therapy for stage II colon cancer, which could provide some guidance for stage IIIA colorectal cancer.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with similar staging and lymph node involvement, potentially useful for comparative purposes despite being a different organ.

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche

suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal patterns, which might offer insights into similar morphological patterns despite being a different organ.

suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with desmoplastic stromal reaction and perineural invasion, which might offer insights into similar histological features.

warnings.message: Biomarqueur non reconnu — vérifier l'orthographe IHC.

differential_diagnosis.diagnosis: Colon adenocarcinoma with acinar variant features
key_supporting: Synthesis_b noted grade heterogeneity and subtle acinar variants in all slides.
Prominent nucleoli and clumped chromatin suggest higher-grade features.
Suspected lymphovascular invasion and desmoplastic stromal reaction are consistent with advanced disease.
key_against: Synthesis_a did not report grade heterogeneity or acinar variants.
No clear evidence of visceral peritoneal involvement or direct extension into adjacent organs.

key_supporting: Synthesis_a and synthesis_b both identified a glandular pattern as the dominant feature.
Coarse chromatin and suspected lymphovascular invasion are present.
key_against: Grade heterogeneity and acinar variant features suggest a higher grade.
Prominent nucleoli and clumped chromatin are more indicative of poorly differentiated carcinoma.

key_supporting: Some cribriform areas could be mistaken for signet ring cell features.
key_against: No prominent signet ring cells were identified.
The overall pattern is more consistent with adenocarcinoma.

treatment_implications.adjuvant_therapy: Consider adjuvant chemotherapy for pT3N0 colon cancer (NCCN CRC v2.2024). Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment.
treatment_implications.immunotherapy: MSI-H / dMMR: pembrolizumab 1L per KEYNOTE-177; MMR IHC pending
treatment_implications.clinical_note: Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion required before initiating treatment. These recommendations are based on pathologic findings only and do not substitute for full clinical evaluation.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the grade heterogeneity and subtle acinar variant features noted in synthesis_b, the primary diagnosis has been revised to 'Colon adenocarcinoma with acinar variant features' with a grade of G3. This revision incorporates the higher-grade features observed in the tumor. The margin status is described as close but not involved in some areas based on synthesis_b. The differential diagnosis includes moderately differentiated colon adenocarcinoma, but the presence of acinar variants and grade heterogeneity supports the higher-grade classification. Further IHC analysis with the recommended panel will help confirm the diagnosis.

literature.key_findings: The provided TCGA cases do not directly correspond to colorectal adenocarcinoma but instead represent various pancreatic neoplasms. The clinical context specifies a colorectal adenocarcinoma in the hepatic flexure of the colon, which is not accurately reflected in the similar cases. The ASCO guideline update for stage II colon cancer provides relevant clinical recommendations for adjuvant therapy in this setting.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, grade II-III

Patient: female, 71y. PDAC head of pancreas, 28mm. Multifocal involvement. Grade heterogeneity II-III. Desmoplastic stromal reaction, dense. Perineural invasion present. Margin: R1 posterior 0.6mm. Lymph nodes: 3/16 positive. pT2 pN1. Outcome: 5y OS 22% reported in matche
suggested_papers.relevance: This case represents a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with similar histological features that could serve as a differential diagnostic consideration.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
suggested_papers.relevance: This case describes a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal involvement, which might be considered in the differential diagnosis despite the primary focus being colorectal cancer.

debate_rounds.argument: Considering the grade heterogeneity and conflicting histopathologist findings, a revision to G2 was necessary. The cribriform pattern, while noted, is not exclusively indicative of G3 in colorectal adenocarcinoma. The presence of poorly differentiated areas in one ROI suggests a possibility of G3, but the overall sample predominantly shows moderately differentiated areas. The margin assessment also showed discrepancies, but the closest assessment was 'close but not involved'. The recommended IHC panel includes markers specific to colorectal adenocarcinoma to further confirm the diagnosis.
